title: RhetorLint Analysis Result
description: The result of analyzing a passage of text for rhetorical tells. RhetorLint marks countable manipulation in the WORDS; it never claims to read the person, and it never adjudicates whether a claim is factually true.

density.description: The headline metric: how thickly the text is marked.

strip.description: Deterministic de-spun version: adverbial spin (intensifiers, deniable adverbs) removed and agentless passives flagged with [who?]. Verb-phrase hedges and other tells are left marked, not paraphrased. On-device, no model required.

rewrite.description: OPTIONAL plain-truth paraphrase. Requires a pluggable model adapter; null when only the deterministic core ran. Never fabricate this in the core.

ruleId.description: family.tell — the verbatim taxonomy label, e.g. 'agency-hiding.deleted-subject'.

family.description: One coarse family. The six SemEval-2023 parents plus RhetorLint's structural extension.

technique.description: Fine-grained technique label, aligned verbatim to SemEval-2023 (Piskorski et al.) where one maps, e.g. 'Loaded Language', 'Obfuscation, Intentional Vagueness, Confusion', 'False Dilemma / No Choice', 'Whataboutism'.

actual.description: The real, visible phrase that was marked. Every mark MUST point at text a reader can see.

note.description: A one-line, phrase-level explanation of what the language is doing. Describes the WORDS, not the speaker's intent.

expected.description: Suggested plain-truth phrasing(s) for this mark.

confidence.description: Rule-level confidence. Heuristic — not a probability of deception. RhetorLint detects language patterns, not lies.

level.description: Severity band. Maps to SARIF result.level. Default 'info' — a mark is an observation, not an accusation.
